questions.prompt: Q3: hfe is found from output characteristics by measuring:
questions.explanation: hfe = ΔIC/ΔIB with VCE held constant. Read IC at two adjacent IB curves at the Q-point VCE.

questions.prompt: Q3: The DC load line x-intercept is:
questions.explanation: DC x-intercept: when IC=0, VCE = VCC = 15V. All supply voltage appears across the transistor.

questions.prompt: Q3: The AC load line is steeper than DC because:
questions.explanation: AC: RAC = RC (CE bypasses RE). DC: RDC = RC+RE. Since RC < RC+RE, the AC slope is steeper.
Q4 states: 'Common-Emitter h-parameter model for Q1'. CE provides voltage gain.
CC (emitter follower) has Av ≈ 1. It's a buffer — it preserves voltage while providing impedance transformation.
CC has Rin = hic + (1+|hfc|)RE ≈ 1100 + 52×5000 ≈ 228 kΩ. Very high — barely loads stage 1.
CC has Rout ≈ hic/(1+|hfc|) = 1100/51 ≈ 22 Ω. Very low — can drive low-impedance loads.

questions.prompt: Q4: Overall input impedance is determined by:
questions.explanation: Overall Rin = Rin of the first stage = Rin1 = 1 kΩ. The source sees only stage 1's input.

questions.prompt: Q4: Overall output impedance is determined by:
questions.explanation: Overall Rout = Rout of the last stage = Rout2 ≈ 22 Ω. The load sees only stage 2's output.

questions.prompt: Q3.4: When IB swings below 0 (cutoff), what happens?
questions.explanation: At cutoff: IC=0. VCE rises to the AC load line x-intercept = VCEQ + ICQ×RAC = 8 + 5.44 = 13.44V.
